description_long: This is a rustic wooden bucket with a cylindrical body constructed from vertical wooden staves, bound together by two prominent black metal hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood has a natural, slightly uneven texture, with visible grain and a dark brown finish, giving it an aged, hand-crafted appearance. The bucket features a thick, curved wooden handle attached to the side, secured by metal brackets, which allows for easy carrying. The rim of the bucket is irregular and slightly jagged, suggesting it was crafted with traditional tools. The interior appears hollow and unfinished, suitable for holding water or other materials. There are no visible labels, markings, or decorative elements, emphasizing its utilitarian design.
description: A rustic wooden bucket with black metal hoops and a thick, curved wooden handle, featuring a rough, hand-crafted appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open top, wooden staves, and both metal hoops clearly, with the handle mostly hidden.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thick, curved wooden handle attached to the bucket and the full profile of the metal hoops.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the attachment point of the handle and the back curvature of the bucket, with the hoops and wood grain visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handle's curve and attachment, as well as the bucket's cylindrical form and metal reinforcements.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: black metal hoops at top and bottom
